evidence: The amber query vector on the dial is the clear focus; the periwinkle keys and lilac score bars are supporting context that covary with it.
A single coherent system (one dial plus a row of four score bars) is presented, well under the four-novel-element ceiling.
q sits on the amber arrow, k1..k4 label their tips, and each bar/readout is captioned k1..k4 directly above it; no legend or swatch key exists.
The only text is the short instruction 'Turn q until it lines up with a key.', the 'q = 90 deg' readout, and object labels — all annotating visible referents.
Labels, the degree readout, and numeric dot-product values are crisp and readable at all three viewport widths including 375px.
The dial, all key tips, the q label, and the full bar row sit inside the frame with margin at 375, 768, and 1280 px.
No text overlaps; the q label repositions to avoid the arrow (e.g. beside k1 in aligned frame) and bar captions clear their bars.
Query is amber (attention), keys are periwinkle (observed), score bars are lilac (parameters/derived), labels are cream — token semantics held consistently across frames.
Every element — vectors, dial circle, bars, readouts — carries propositional content; no decorative ornament is present.

Covariation is legible from stills: aligned-with-k1 shows k1 bar peaked at 1.00, anti-aligned shows k1 at -1.00 minimum, and mid-drag shows k1 (0.98) leading k2, matching the intended query-direction-to-score-height mapping.
Each score bar sits directly under its captioned key and above its numeric readout, keeping related items contiguous.
The frame set shows a continuous sweep of the query (90->30->20->200 deg) with bars morphing accordingly rather than cutting.
Generous margins and a clear top-to-bottom reading order (dial, then readout, then bar row) hold across all viewport widths.
